page.content: The results indicate that as time progresses from 0 to 90 minutes, there is a noticeable decrease in the observed effects, as reflected in the values of 3.02 ± 0.12 at 0 minutes, tapering to 2.85 ± 0.10 by 90 minutes.

page.content: This trend suggests a potential time-dependent decay in the additive effects of quantitative trait loci (QTL), which could have implications for the interpretation of genetic influence over time. Furthermore, the data highlights the varying levels of minor allele frequency (MAF) across different scenarios, underscoring the importance of genetic variation in the observed effects. The insights gleaned from this table may inform future research directions and enhance our understanding of the dynamic interplay between genetic factors and temporal variables in various biological contexts.
